metadata.architecture: Hosted MCP Server with Neon Native Auth + Per-Org Isolation

prerequisites.prerequisite: Organizations and org_id columns in place

user_flow.step: 2. User navigates to Settings > MCP Integration
3. Dashboard generates config with user's auth token
5. Claude Code connects to hosted MCP server
6. MCP server validates Neon Auth token
7. MCP server extracts org_id, filters all queries
8. User sees only their organization's test data

batch.name: HTTP/SSE Transport Setup
batch.description: Create MCP server with HTTP transport using Hono (lightweight, Vercel-friendly)

file.purpose: NPM package with MCP SDK, Hono, and Neon Auth
HTTP server entry point with SSE transport

verification_steps.step: npm run dev to start server locally

batch.name: Neon Auth Token Validation
batch.description: Implement Neon Auth token validation and user/org context extraction

neon_auth_info.note: Neon Auth tokens are JWTs signed by Neon's auth service
Token contains user email which maps to dashboard_users
We look up org membership from our database

file.purpose: Neon Auth token validation and context extraction
Hono middleware for auth context injection

environment_variables.var: Neon Auth JWKS endpoint for token verification

verification_steps.step: Get a valid Neon Auth token from dashboard
Test token validation: curl -H "Authorization: Bearer TOKEN" localhost:3001/health
Verify user lookup returns correct org_id

batch.name: Org-Filtered Database Queries
batch.description: Database connection and query utilities with automatic org filtering

file.purpose: Org-scoped query functions for MCP tools

verification_steps.step: Test getExecutions with valid org_id returns data
Test getExecutions with wrong org_id returns empty
Verify all queries include org filter

batch.name: Core MCP Tools
batch.description: Implement the primary query tools for test execution data

tool.name: get_executions
tool.description: List test executions with optional filters

tool.name: get_execution_details
tool.description: Get detailed info about a specific execution including all test results

tool.name: search_tests
tool.description: Search for tests by name or file path

tool.name: get_test_history
tool.description: Get execution history for a specific test

file.purpose: MCP Server class with tool registration

verification_steps.step: Test get_execution_details with wrong org's ID returns error

batch.name: Analysis and Metrics Tools
batch.description: Add failure analysis, metrics, and trend tools

tools_to_add.tool: Returns failed tests with AI context

batch.implementation_note: Follow same pattern as Batch 4 - all queries use authContext.organizationId

batch.name: Flakiness and Artifacts
batch.description: Flaky test detection and artifact URL generation

tools_to_add.tool: List artifacts for a test result

batch.artifact_security_note: Verify artifact belongs to user's org before generating signed URL: JOIN through test_result -> execution -> organization_id

batch.name: Dashboard MCP Settings Page
batch.description: Create UI in dashboard for users to get their MCP configuration

file.pseudocode:
"use client" import { useState, useEffect } from "react" import { useNeonAuthUI } from "@neondatabase/auth/react/ui" import { Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardContent } from "@/components/ui/card" import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button" import { Tabs, TabsContent, TabsList, TabsTrigger } from "@/components/ui/tabs" import { Copy, Check, RefreshCw, ExternalLink } from "lucide-react" import { Alert, AlertDescription } from "@/components/ui/alert" export default function MCPSettingsPage() { const { session, getAccessToken } = useNeonAuthUI() const [token, setToken] = useState(null) const [copied, setCopied] = useState(false) const [testing, setTesting] = useState(false) const [testResult, setTestResult] = useState<"success" | "error" | null>(null) const MCP_SERVER_URL = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_MCP_SERVER_URL || "https://e2e-dashboard-mcp.vercel.app" useEffect(() => { async function fetchToken() { if (session) { const accessToken = await getAccessToken() setToken(accessToken) } } fetchToken() }, [session]) const config = { mcpServers: { "e2e-dashboard": { url: `${MCP_SERVER_URL}/mcp`, transport: "sse", headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token || ""}` } } } } const configString = JSON.stringify(config, null, 2) async function copyConfig() { await navigator.clipboard.writeText(configString) setCopied(true) setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 2000) } async function testConnection() { setTesting(true) setTestResult(null) try { const res = await fetch(`${MCP_SERVER_URL}/health`, { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` } }) setTestResult(res.ok ? "success" : "error") } catch { setTestResult("error") } finally { setTesting(false) } } return (

MCP Integration

Connect Claude Code to access your test data directly

Your Configuration Add this to your ~/.claude.json or project .claude.json file 
              {configString}
{testResult === "success" && ( Connection successful! MCP server is reachable. )} {testResult === "error" && ( Connection failed. Check your network and try again. )}
Available Tools These tools will be available in Claude Code after setup
Setup Guide Click the copy button above to copy your MCP configuration Open or create ~/.claude.json and paste the configuration Restart Claude Code to load the new MCP server Ask Claude to "get recent test failures" or "search for login tests"
) } function ToolItem({ name, description }: { name: string; description: string }) { return (
{name} {description}
) } function Step({ number, title, children }: { number: number; title: string; children: React.ReactNode }) { return (
{number}

{title}

{children}

) }

batch.navigation_update: Add "MCP Integration" link to settings navigation

verification_steps.step: Copy config and verify it's valid JSON

batch.name: Deployment and Documentation
batch.description: Deploy MCP server to Vercel and create user documentation

deployment_steps.step: 1. Create new Vercel project for mcp-server
2. Configure environment variables: - DATABASE_URL - NEON_AUTH_JWKS_URL - R2_* variables (optional, for artifacts)

file.content_spec:
# E2E Dashboard MCP Server
Hosted MCP server with Neon Auth authentication for the E2E Test Dashboard.
## Features - Per-organization data isolation - Neon Auth token validation - 12 tools for test data access - R2 artifact URL signing
## Setup for Users
1. Log into the E2E Dashboard 2. Go to Settings > MCP Integration 3. Copy your configuration 4. Add to ~/.claude.json 5. Restart Claude Code
## Available Tools - get_executions, get_execution_details - search_tests, get_test_history - get_failed_tests, analyze_failure - get_dashboard_metrics, get_trends - get_flaky_tests, get_flakiness_summary - list_artifacts, get_artifact_url
## Environment Variables - DATABASE_URL - Neon PostgreSQL connection - NEON_AUTH_JWKS_URL - Neon Auth JWKS endpoint - R2_* - Cloudflare R2 credentials (optional)
